data.title: FCC chair pleased with Skydance vow to make changes at CBS
data.description:
The chair of the Federal Communications Commission said on Thursday he is pleased with commitments that Skydance has made to make serious changes at CBS under a proposed merger with Paramount , but said the agency has not made a decision on the tie-up.

CBS parent Paramount needs approval from the FCC for an $8.4-billion merger with Skydance Media. FCC Chair Brendan Carr said after a commission meeting the agency continues to review the deal and praised commitments to end diversity programs.

Skydance has agreed to have an ombudsman in place for at least two years to evaluate complaints about bias in CBS News' programming if the deal is approved.

"I was very pleased to see Skydance put in a filing that says if this deal goes through they are committing to serious changes at CBS. I think that would be a good thing," Carr said. "They have committed to addressing bias issues. They've committed to embracing fact-based journalism."

Carr cited polls that Americans' trust in national programs has fallen to historic lows.

This month, Paramount agreed to pay $16 million to settle a lawsuit filed by Trump claiming the CBS news program "60 Minutes" deceptively edited an interview with former Vice President Kamala Harris that the network broadcast in October.

The FCC did not make a decision by the 180-day informal deadline in mid-May and Carr previously denied Trump's lawsuit was a factor.

Trump and CBS formally agreed on Tuesday to the dismissal of his lawsuit, according to a court filing.

Skydance and its investors plan to acquire National Amusements, which holds the family's controlling stake in Paramount. Skydance will subsequently be merged into Paramount, with its CEO David Ellison becoming Paramount's next chief executive.

data.title: Did You Lose Money on Neogen Corporation (NEOG)? Levi & Korsinsky Urges Investors to Act Before September 16, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Neogen Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158016&wire=1&utm_campaign=28

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Neogen Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between January 5, 2023 and June 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants issued a series of materially false and misleading statements which led investors to believe that the integration with the Food Safety Division of the 3M Company was progressing much better than it actually was. In addition, even when the Company was forced to reveal that certain "inefficiencies" arose as a result of the integration, defendants downplayed them and assured investors that they were fully aware and committed to resolving them quickly.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Neogen Corporation stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158016&wire=1&utm_campaign=28 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Chipotle Faces Short-Term Pressures, But Long-Term Opportunities — Market Talk
data.description: Chipotle says macro pressure impacted the lower-income consumer in 2Q, but BTIG points out the company's restaurant margin was modestly better at 27.4%. Long term, the company expects to return to mid-single-digit comp growth, but a macro tailwind might be necessary to get to that level, analysts Peter Saleh and Ben Parente say. They are "cautiously optimistic" that increases in loyalty members, catering efforts and limited-time offers will drive a meaningful change in comparable sales entering next year. Chipotle is off 14%. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

data.title: WTI Midland, Oseberg ease
data.description:
U.S. WTI Midland and North Sea Oseberg crude differentials eased on Thursday after lower offers on both grades.

PLATTS WINDOW

* Indications are on a free-on-board (FOB) basis unless marked as cost, insurance and freight (CIF) or delivered at place (DAP).

* WTI Midland: Phillips 66 offered August 12-16 CIF Rotterdam at dated Brent plus $1.45, down 40 cents from a deal on Wednesday and equal to dated Brent plus 50 cents on an FOB basis, according to Reuters calculations.

* Oseberg: Equinor offered for August 11-13 at dated Brent plus $2.20. That was lower than an offer on Wednesday.

data.title: Mattel Aims to Partner with 'Global Leaders' to Support Its Brands — Market Talk
data.description: Barbie is making her way back to the silver screen, this time in animation. Mattel is partnering with Illumination--the studio behind the "Despicable Me" franchise and "The Super Mario Bros. Movie"--on the new film, which isn't yet dated. In addition to its partnership with Illumination, Mattel recently tapped "Wicked"-filmmaker Jon M. Chu to direct a live-action Hot Wheels movie, and began collaborating with OpenAI to develop AI-powered toys and experiences around its portfolio of brands. "We are partnering with best-in-class, global leaders in their domain," Mattel CEO Ynon Kreiz says. These partnerships, he says, will "reimagine how far our brands can go." Mattel shares fall 13% after dialing back its full-year outlook. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: MGIC Investment Increases Dividend by 15%
data.description:
By Connor Hart

MGIC Investment raised its quarterly dividend to 15 cents a share from 13 cents.

The new payout, equal to 60 cents a year, represents an annual yield of about 2.3% based on Wednesday's closing price of $25.74.

The Milwaukee provider of mortgage insurance said Thursday that the higher dividend will be paid on Aug. 21 to shareholders of record as of Aug. 7.

MGIC last increased its quarterly dividend about a year ago.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Is Investigating Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMC) And Encourages Stockholders to Connect
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Lockheed Martin Corporation ("Lockheed" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Lockheed securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/LMT.

Investigation Details

On July 22, 2025, Lockheed released its second quarter 2025 financial results, reporting sharply lower second-quarter earnings, including $1.6 billion in program losses. The Company disclosed it was forced to recognize $950 million in losses related to its Aeronautics Classified program due to "design, integration, and test challenges, as well as other performance issues" as well as "significant changes to its processes and testing approach." The Company also reported $570 million in losses on its Canadian Maritime Helicopter Program due in part to providing "additional mission capabilities, enhanced logistical support, fleet life extension, and revised expectations regarding flight hours." The Company further reported a $95 million charge related to its Turkish Utility Helicopter Program due to the "current status of the program." The Company stated it is in "ongoing discussion" with its customers regarding a potential "restructure" of certain contractual terms and conditions. Following this news, Lockheed's stock dropped.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Lockheed securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/LMT. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC:

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

Contact

SOURCE: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

data.title: Europe's CISPE challenges Broadcom's $69 billion VMware deal in EU court
data.description:
European Union judges could review chipmaker Broadcom's $69 billion acquisition of cloud computing firm VMWare, after an antitrust complaint opposed the bloc's approval of one of the biggest takeovers in the technology industry.

The Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe, or CISPE, has filed a formal appeal before the European General Court seeking an annulment of the European Commission's approval of the deal, the organisation said on Thursday.

Broadcom had wrapped up the purchase of VMWare in November 2023, after intense scrutiny from regulators globally forced the company to delay the closing date thrice.

The European Commission's official summary of its approval decision was published in May, acknowledging that the acquisition posed significant risks to competition.

"However, it failed to impose any conditions on Broadcom to prevent a concentration of dominance or to mitigate the potential abuse of such a position," CISPE said.

The European Commission did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

CISPE said it has "consistently raised alarms" with the Commission over Broadcom's unfair software licensing practices, but no substantive action has been taken to support either European cloud service providers or their customers.

VMware's dominance of software in the virtualisation market "means that unfair new licensing terms enforced by Broadcom affect almost every European organisation using cloud technology", said Francisco Mingorance, Secretary General of CISPE.

"Broadcom strongly disagrees with these allegations," a company spokesperson said, adding that the deal was approved after a "thorough merger review process, and we will uphold the commitments made to the Commission at that time."

The deal, one of the biggest globally when announced in May 2022, was part of Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's efforts to boost the chipmaker's software business.

data.title: Toronto Stocks Nudge Higher, Tracking Toward New High
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Shares on Canada's main exchange were little changed Thursday, building only slightly on a fresh high for the market as earnings season gathers steam.

In midday trading, the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index was 25.29 points higher at 27441.70. It touched a new intraday high of 27479.01 earlier in the session.

The finance sector was the biggest driver, countering weakness in mining, consumer services and technology services.

The blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 was up 2.28 points at 1631.81.

Heavily-weighted FirstService and Waste Connections topped the leaders board in the wake of quarterly results, while Teck Resources was among the day's losers following its own numbers and lowered production guidance.

The six major banks were each higher, led by a 0.5% gain by National Bank of Canada and 0.3% advance for Bank of Nova Scotia.

Other market movers:

FirstService rose 11% to C$270.19 after the property services company's second-quarter profit and sales beat expectations.

Waste Connections gained 2.3% to C$256.42 after the waste management company's second-quarter results met expectations and its financial guidance for the year remained near its earlier targets despite what it said were growing headwinds.

Loblaw was up 1.4% at C$220.55 after the grocery giant's second-quarter revenue and profit rose as same-store traffic, basket size and item count all increased from a year earlier.

Mullen fell 4.5% to C$13.37 after the trucking company's adjusted earnings for the latest quarter missed expectations while freight demand slowed and pricing pressure continued to affect customers.

Teck was 5.8% weaker at C$49.43 after the miner scaled back its target for annual copper production due to ongoing development work at its mines and potential additional downtime.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Priyanka Capital Announces Name Change and Technical Report Filing
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Priyanka Capital Inc. (the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has changed its name to "Pecoy Copper Corp." effective July 23, 2025 (the "Name Change"). The Company has changed its name in connection with the proposed reverse take-over transaction with Pecoy Copper Limited, the details of which are included in the Company's news release dated July 3, 2025.

In connection with the Name Change, the new CUSIP 70529A102 and ISIN CA70529A1021 have been assigned to the common shares of the Company. The Name Change was approved by the board of directors of the Company in accordance with the Company's governing corporate legislation, the Business Corporations Act (British Columbia), and the Company's constating documents. Common share certificates bearing the previous company name "Priyanka Capital Inc." continue to be valid in the settlement of trades and will only be replaced with certificates bearing the new name upon transfer. The Company is not requesting, and shareholders are not required to, exchange their existing share certificates for new certificates bearing the new Company name.

Technical Report

The Company is also pleased to announce that, further to its news release dated June 11, 2025, the Company has filed a technical report titled "NI 43-101 Technical Report Mineral Resource Estimate - Pecoy Project, Condesuyos Province, Arequipa - Peru" (the "Technical Report") under the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. The Technical Report was prepared in accordance with National Instrument 43-101 - Standards of Disclosure for Mineral Projects and was authored by an independent Qualified Person, María del Carmen Muñoz Lizarve, MAIG, of Mining Plus Canada Consulting Ltd.

About the Company

The Company is a reporting issuer in the Provinces of British Columbia and Alberta. The Company's shares are not currently listed on any stock exchange and it is presently engaged in identifying and evaluating potential business opportunities.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS

Robert Dubeau, President, CEO and Director

Suite 1500, 1055 West Georgia Street, Vancouver, BC V6E 4N7

Telephone: 778 837 8550

Email: bobbydubeau@yahoo.ca

Although the Company believes the expectations expressed in such forward-looking statements are based on reasonable assumptions, such statements are not guarantees of future performance and actual results may differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements. Investors are cautioned that any such statements are not guarantees of future performance and actual results or developments may differ materially from those projected in the forward-looking statements. data.title: LVMH CFO: Group Needs to Ensure That It Maintains High Level of Margins
Keurig Dr Pepper Sees Interest in Wellness-Oriented Categories — Market Talk
data.description: Keurig Dr Pepper CEO Tim Cofer says a consumer shift toward health and wellness is the single biggest trend impacting the beverages industry. Consumers are increasingly looking for beverages with protein, hydration, zero sugar options, fiber and gut health benefits, he says. The shift has resulted in strength in the company's energy and sports hydration businesses, he says. Keurig Dr Pepper plans to continue capitalizing on the shift with the launch of a prebiotic carbonated soft drink this quarter, and will continue looking for opportunities to offer protein drinks, he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1244 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper CEO Tim Cofer says a consumer shift toward health and wellness is the single biggest trend impacting the beverages industry. Consumers are increasingly looking for beverages with protein, hydration, zero sugar options, fiber and gut health benefits, he says. The shift has resulted in strength in the company's energy and sports hydration businesses, he says. Keurig Dr Pepper plans to continue capitalizing on the shift with the launch of a prebiotic carbonated soft drink this quarter, and will continue looking for opportunities to offer protein drinks, he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1243 ET - American Airlines is taking a conservative approach to its guidance for the remainder of the year despite indications of improving U.S. travel demand, Melius Research analysts Conor Cunningham and Patrick Coleman say in a research note. Investors don't seem happy with the outlook, with shares now down 8.3% at $11.63. Delta Air Lines and United Airlines have both called out a sequential uptick in July bookings from June as the economic backdrop becomes less uncertain, the analysts say. American is reporting a similar rate of change, but taking a more cautious view of how it will unfold in the back half of 2025, they say. The carrier was disproportionately hurt by the slowdown in demand earlier this year given its outsized exposure to domestic travel, the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1233 ET - Lamb Weston's board is sending a signal that they're going to be disciplined, Bernstein Research's Alexia Howard said. The french-fry maker in June appointed six new independent directors to its board as part of a cooperation agreement with activist investor Jana Partners. In a sign of its discipline, the board unanimously elected to receive FY26 annual cash retainer in shares of restricted stock. Lamb Weston's annual incentive plan for the fiscal year will include a free cash flow target and its long-term incentive plan will include a return on invested capital related metric. "This suggests they're all going to be mindful of using cash and returns to shareholders," Howard says. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1221 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper continues to see strength across its four energy drink brands, which take up a combined 7% market share in the overall category, up from less than 1% a few years ago, CEO Tim Cofer says during a call with analysts. That share is on track to grow to double digits as the company allocates more resources to the category, he says. "This has been probably the best example of portfolio transformation in the last few years," he says. Other beverage companies have similarly suggested an increased focus on energy drinks in recent quarters amid popularity with younger consumers. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1217 ET - Hasbro's 2Q results more than delivered the bull case, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a research note. That's partly because the maker of Nerf blasters and Play-Doh posted better-than-expected sales and raised its outlook for the remainder of the year, they write. Hasbro also issued positive comments regarding its set-up for 2026, citing a strong entertainment slate as new "Star Wars," "Spider-Man" and "Avengers" movies are set to hit theaters, as well as new products across popular brands such as Beyblade and Peppa Pig. JPM raises its price target by 25% to $94. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1216 ET - Chipotle Mexican Grill's shares are under pressure after the company logged a 4% drop in 2Q comparable sales, a decline that came despite visits being up 0.7% for the quarter, according to Placer.ai data. The comps decrease may be explained by the fact that despite the growth in visits, which outpaced the wider fast-casual segment, average visits per Chipotle location was down 6%, according to a Placer.ai report last week. Despite that per-location overhang, the chain's overall visit growth has helped it capture a growing share of industrywide fast-casual visits, the report says. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1204 ET - Mattel is optimistic regarding the toy industry's outlook for the remainder of the year, telling investors on a call that retailers are simply delaying orders, not ordering down, and that inventory levels are healthy. Its outlook may say otherwise, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a research note. The maker of Uno cards and Barbie dolls reinstated its annual guidance, now calling for net sales to grow 1% to 3% this year, compared with a prior forecast of 2% to 3%. Weakness in Barbie and Fisher-Price hurt results in 2Q, but Mattel expects declines to get "less worse" in the back half of the year. This outlook is mixed, the analysts say, "considering Barbie and Hot Wheels drive the proverbial Mattel dream bus." Shares fall 12%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1202 ET - Tesla's long-term upside is largely predicated on its plans for lower cost cars, autonomous vehicles and AI products. But it offered few updates to these critical releases with its 2Q results, Truist analysts say. "At a minimum, considering they are already supposedly in production, we expected the company to describe aspects of its new lower-cost models (cost, features, estimated production, etc.) Alas, management offered no such details," the analysts say, adding that the company also said little about its autonomous vehicles, Optimus robot or Dojo supercomputer. Tesla also didn't provide any meaningful future financial guidance, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1201 ET - Copper prices slip in European evening trade as market participants keep a close eye on supply chain shifts ahead of President Trump's tariffs, with ships carrying the metal racing to reach U.S. ports before August 1. LME three-month copper is down 0.6% to $9,870.50 a metric ton, but remains up 2% on week. "With the August 1 deadline for copper tariffs ahead of us, we see risks skewed to the downside as the pull to the U.S. stops and the LME price could potentially be dragged down," analysts at RBC Capital Markets say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

1157 ET - Keurig Dr Pepper says it gained market share in the U.S. beverages category last quarter with a greater variety of new flavors in its carbonated soft drinks. The beverage giant's Dr Pepper BlackBerry now ranks as the top new product in the category this year, while 7UP is gaining momentum with flavors like tropical and "endless summer," CEO Tim Cofer says. Americans are eager to try new drinks, with about three quarters of Gen Z consumers trying a new beverage every month, he says.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1152 ET - Summer sales, protein offers and discounts are among the initiatives UBS analysts believe will push Chipotle Mexican Grill into a better second half after reporting a 4% same-store sales decline in its most recent quarter. Despite macro headwinds, UBS analysts expect Chipotle to see stronger sales momentum if it continues strategies such as introducing three limited-time protein options, pushing under $10 chicken offerings and introducing a new college rewards program. The company should lean into its catering business, digital users and marketing to gain momentum, analysts say. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1149 ET - To fund its long-term bets in AI and autonomous vehicles, Tesla is going to need cash. But the company only generated $146 million in free cash flow in 2Q, down from $1.34 billion the previous year. That could be a warning sign for its future cash reserves, say Truist analysts in a research note. "Considering the turbulence from tariffs, the reduction in consumer & regulatory credits, and ongoing capex needs, management acknowledged that the next few quarters could get worse, and potentially drain some of the company's $37b cash position," the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Taurus Gold Corp. Announces Appointment of Gabriel Kabazo to the Board of Directors and as Chief Financial Officer; Resignation of Meghan Brown
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - July 24, 2025) - Taurus Gold Corp.  (OTC Pink: TARGF) (the "Company") is pleased to announce the appointment of Mr. Gabriel Kabazo to its Board of Directors and as Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately.

Mr. Kabazo, 52, is a seasoned finance and operations executive with over 25 years of experience in accounting, finance, and IT operations across both public and private companies. He currently serves as Chief Executive Officer of Fort Technology Inc. , a position he assumed in July 2025. In addition, he serves as Chief Financial Officer of Femto Technologies Inc. (OTCID: FMTOF), Plantify Foods Inc. , and Starmet Ventures Inc. .

Mr. Kabazo holds a B.A. in Accounting and Economics from Tel Aviv University (1997), received his CPA designation in Israel in 1999, and earned an MBA in Finance from the Sauder School of Business at the University of British Columbia in 2006.

The Company also announces the resignation of Ms. Meghan Brown from the Board of Directors. The Board extends its sincere gratitude to Ms. Brown for her dedicated service and valuable contributions and wishes her continued success in her future endeavors.

Neither the Canadian Securities Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

About Taurus Gold

Taurus Gold is a growth-oriented mineral exploration company with a 51% interest in the Charlotte gold-silver property in the Yukon. The Charlotte property covers 23 square kilometres and is accessible by road within the established Dawson Range mining district. The Company's shares trade on the CSE under the symbol TAUR.

For more information please contact:

Robert Sim, Director and Interim Chief Executive Officer

t: 604 617 4803

e: info@taurusgold.ca

Forward-looking Information

This news release contains "forward-looking information" or "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of Canadian securities legislation. Forward-looking information and forward-looking statements, while based on management's best estimates and assumptions, are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause the actual results, performance, or achievements of Taurus Gold to be materially different from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking information or forward-looking statements. Although Taurus Gold has attempted to identify important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in forward-looking information and forward-looking statements, there may be other factors that cause results not to be as anticipated, estimated or intended. There can be no assurance that such information or statements will prove to be accurate, as actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such information or statements. The forward-looking statements and forward-looking information are made as of the date hereof and Taurus Gold disclaims, except as required by applicable securities laws, any obligation to update any such factors or to publicly announce the result of any revisions to any of the forward-looking statements or forward-looking information contained herein to reflect future results.
